sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 71:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 71:1
items.html: The upper light is captured in the Partzuf only to the extent of the reflected light that rises from the screen in the Partzuf, and its measure depends on the measure of the coarseness that there is in phase four (See answers 101 and 2). For this reason, the giver needs the excessive coarseness in the lower one. Conversely, the receiver needs the most refined vessel, so that the light may clothe in it, meaning to have equivalence of form between the light and the vessel as the disparity of form between them removes the light from the vessels (See answers 16 and 140).
